title: Cannot dereference type
explanation: This error occurs when you try to use the dereference operator (*) on a type that does not implement the Deref trait. Only types like references, Box, Rc, Arc, and other smart pointers can be dereferenced.

fixes.code:
let x: i32 = 42;
let y = x; // i32 implements Copy, no dereference needed

fixes.description: Dereference a reference, not the value itself
Implement Deref for custom smart pointer types

tip: The * operator calls the deref() method. Rust also has automatic dereferencing (deref coercion) which handles most cases for you.
